channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl brings Arab and Israeli scientists together
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — It soars over the borders and divisions of the war-torn Middle East, bringing Arab and Israeli academics together. An unlikely symbol of cooperation, the barn owl is now getting global attention.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Several people are feared dead in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Several people were feared dead and many more injured in a stampede early Wednesday as tens of thousands of Hindus rushed to take ritual baths in sacred rivers at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in northern India.
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities in Asia and around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Lunar New Year festivals and prayers marked the start of the Year of the Snake around Asia and farther afield on Wednesday — including in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump plans to sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday will sign the Laken Riley Act into law as his administration's first piece of legislation. It mandates the detention and potential deportation of people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: F-35 fighter jet crashes at Eielson Air Force Base in Alaska, pilot taken to hospital
item.description: An F-35 fighter jet crashed Tuesday after an in-flight malfunction at Eielson Air Force Base in Alaska, sending the pilot to the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump order aims to end federal support for gender transitions for those under 19
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order aimed at cutting federal support for gender transitions for people under age 19, his latest move to roll back protections for transgender people across the country.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, GEOFF MULVIHILL and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump fires two Democratic commissioners of agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace
item.description: President Donald Trump has fired two of the three Democratic commissioners of the federal agency that enforces civil rights laws in the workplace, an unprecedented move aimed at implementing his crackdown on certain diversity and gender rights policies.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Olson And Claire Savage, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Head Start and Medicaid providers hit glitches as Trump freezes federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump paused federal grants and loans in a far-reaching directive, early childhood education centers and states discovered Tuesday they could no longer access money they rely on to provide care for some of the nation's neediest families and children.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carnivore diet sends man to hospital with hands filled with cholesterol
item.description: The patient was eventually diagnosed with a condition known as xanthelasma — the build-up of cholesterol under the skin.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Firings, freezes and layoffs: A look at Trump's moves against federal employees and programs
item.description: CHAPIN, S.C. (AP) — Changeover among federal government agencies is normal when a new administration comes to Washington, with presidents regularly dismissing appointees selected by predecessors from opposing political parties.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The death toll from a New Year's Eve illegal fireworks explosion in Honolulu climbs to 5
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A fifth person has died from injuries suffered during a massive explosion of illegal fireworks at a Honolulu home over New Year's that prompted Hawaii authorities to reevaluate how they enforce the state's fireworks laws, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in US history hits Kansas, officials say
item.description: The Kansas Department of Health and Environment (KDHE) says Kansas is facing the largest documented tuberculosis outbreak in American history.
item.dc:creator: Colter Robinson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Watch a miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off the central coast of California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Young people 'inspired' by Trump and Musk, UCLA survey finds
item.description: A large number of young people say they are "inspired" by the likes of Elon Musk and Donald Trump and want to be like them, a survey from UCLA found.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Weeks after fire ravaged LA's Pacific Palisades, residents return to dig for mementos
item.description: Scores of Los Angeles residents have returned to the wildfire-ravaged Pacific Palisades neighborhood after officials began letting people drive back into the scorched community.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Weber, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chicago's 'sanctuary city' status under investigation by feds
item.description: Chicago and other cities like it — notably Denver, New York and Boston, all of which hold sanctuary city status like Chicago — are being investigated by the U.S. House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay area restaurants make list of most romantic in the US, according to OpenTable
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Valentine's Day is just around the corner and finding the perfect restaurant to take the one you love can always be challenging.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Coca-Cola launches new Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pence group uses Trump's criticism against RFK Jr. in new ad
item.description: An advocacy group founded by former Vice President Mike Pence is escalating its fight against Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: ICE snags criminal alien paroled 17 years into life sentence for pregnant woman's murder
item.description: Cesar Polanco, a criminal alien from the Dominican Republic who killed his pregnant girlfriend, has been taken into ICE custody after being granted parole in MA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hispanic community reacts to ICE operations in Austin
item.description: In a part of Austin with deep Latino roots, long-time Hispanic community leader Paul Saldana said he feels for his community.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson brushes aside economic concerns associated with ICE raids
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is brushing aside economic concerns associated with the raids that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officials have carried out in recent days, as Democrats warn that the mass deportations could harm the economy.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Coke jumps on nostalgic trend with new Coca-Cola Orange Cream flavor
item.description: A year after the launch of the short-lived Coca-Cola Spiced, Coke is adding another new flavor to its lineup.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jets agree to terms with Broncos executive Darren Mougey to be their general manager
item.description: The New York Jets' lengthy searches are over, and they're pairing a first-time general manager with a first-time head coach to try to turn things around.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK JR., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Last 4 escaped monkeys are captured in South Carolina after months on the loose
item.description: Authorities in South Carolina said Friday the last four of 43 escaped monkeys have been recaptured after two months living in the woods, weathering a rare snowstorm and being temped back into captivity by peanut butter and jelly sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A resident of the central Oregon city of Bend says he was the person behind some of the googly eyes that appeared on sculptures around the city in recent months and sparked a viral sensation widely covered by news outlets.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4
